memes.description: "The figure of the angel as a symbol of purity and otherworldly goodness in George MacDonald\'s *At the Back of the North Wind*."

memes.label: The Back of the North Wind
memes.description: The transcendent realm beyond suffering in George MacDonald's *At the Back of the North Wind* — a paradise-like country that Diamond visits in his dreams and ultimately enters at death.

memes.label: beauty
memes.description: The transformative power of beauty in George MacDonald's *At the Back of the North Wind*.

memes.label: "Chapter XXXVIII: AT THE BACK OF THE NORTH WIND"
Character Roles
memes.description: Characters grouped by narrative role — protagonists, family members, benefactors, servants, and more.

memes.label: Child Epistemology — Knowing Without Doctrine
memes.description: An analysis of MacDonald's epistemology of childhood — the claim that children, precisely because they lack adult rationality, possess a superior form of knowledge that is intuitive, embodied, and directly receptive to divine truth.

memes.label: Courage
memes.description: A virtue that Diamond learns through his adventures with the North Wind in George MacDonald's *At the Back of the North Wind*, defined not as the absence of fear but as trust in the face of it.

memes.label: Darkness
memes.description: A recurring motif in George MacDonald's *At the Back of the North Wind*, where darkness functions not as a symbol of evil but as a necessary condition for mystery, protection, and the partial revelation of divine light.

memes.label: Diamond
memes.description: The young protagonist of George MacDonald's *At the Back of the North Wind*, a poor coachman's son whose innocence and goodness allow him to journey with the North Wind.

memes.label: Diamond's Father
memes.description: Diamond's hardworking father, a London cabman and coachman whose fortunes rise and fall through the novel, ultimately finding security through his own honesty and the patronage of Mr. Raymond.

memes.label: Diamond's Mother
memes.description: Diamond's practical, anxious mother who shares her husband's economic struggles and endures her son's mysterious nature with quiet faithfulness, eventually finding peace in the country cottage.

memes.label: Dream
memes.description: A central narrative and thematic device in George MacDonald's *At the Back of the North Wind*, in which the boundary between dream and reality is deliberately blurred to convey the novel's spiritual vision that the supernatural is as real as the material world.

relations.label: the country at the back of the north wind

memes.label: Dream and Symbol
memes.description: Entries exploring the dream-like, supernatural, and symbolic dimensions of the North Wind narrative — the threshold spaces where reality and imagination meet.

memes.label: The Drunken Cabman
memes.description: A drunken London cabman living in the same lodging-house as Diamond's family, whose misery and redemption Diamond witnesses — and facilitates — in a single night.

memes.label: Fear
memes.description: A pervasive emotional and moral force in George MacDonald's *At the Back of the North Wind*, experienced by Diamond as a barrier to be overcome through trust in the North Wind and moral growth.

memes.label: Featured Content
memes.description: A showcase of all featured and premium-quality pages in the wiki, representing the highest-quality entries.

memes.label: heaven
memes.description: The dual meaning of heaven as physical sky and spiritual destination in George MacDonald's *At the Back of the North Wind*.

memes.label: Home
memes.description: The concept of home as spiritual sanctuary and moral center — the novel's foundational value, from which all journeys depart and to which all returns tend.

memes.label: Hoxton
memes.description: A working-class district in East London where Mr. Raymond's household relocates after Mr. Coleman's financial ruin.

memes.label: Jim
memes.description: A lame crossing-sweeper boy befriended by Diamond and Nanny, whose kindness transforms his life through the charity of Mr. Raymond.

memes.label: Joy
memes.description: A central emotional register in George MacDonald's *At the Back of the North Wind*, where joy functions as both a natural human response and a spiritual signpost pointing toward the divine.

memes.label: Kindness
memes.description: The active virtue of noticing and alleviating the suffering of others — the novel's central ethical imperative, practiced by Diamond and modeled for the reader.

memes.label: Light
memes.description: A recurring motif in George MacDonald's *At the Back of the North Wind*, functioning as a visual symbol of spiritual presence, beauty, and the boundary between the material and supernatural worlds.

memes.label: London
memes.description: The great Victorian city that serves as the primary urban setting of George MacDonald's *At the Back of the North Wind*, where Diamond's family moves and where he works as a cabman and befriends Nanny and Mr. Raymond.

relations.label: the land beyond the north wind

memes.label: Love
memes.description: The central ethical and theological principle of the novel, manifesting as familial devotion, charitable kindness, romantic reconciliation, and the divine love embodied by North Wind.

relations.label: the country at the back of the north wind

memes.label: Miss Coleman
memes.description: A kind-hearted gentlewoman who is Diamond's father's mistress and benefactor to the family; her illness and romantic history form a significant subplot.

relations.label: the country at the back of the north wind

memes.label: moon
memes.description: The moon as a recurring celestial symbol of cyclical change and the wondrous in George MacDonald's *At the Back of the North Wind*.

memes.label: Moral Universe
memes.description: The moral and spiritual themes that form the philosophical foundation of George MacDonald's novel, with entries organized by conceptual category.

memes.label: Most Referenced Pages
memes.description: The top 20 most referenced pages in the wiki, ranked by total cross-reference count. Reflects the most interconnected topics in the North Wind universe.

memes.label: Mr. Coleman
memes.description: Diamond's father's employer and the owner of the Coleman estate, whose financial ruin from dishonest speculation drives the latter half of the plot.

memes.label: Mr. Dyves
memes.description: A neighbour mentioned in the opening chapter whose garden serves as a point of childhood fascination for Diamond.

memes.label: Mr. Evans
memes.description: A gentleman connected to Miss Coleman who undertakes a speculative business venture, suffers exposure and exhaustion in a storm, and is rescued through the providential intervention of North Wind.

memes.label: Mr. Raymond
memes.description: A kind-hearted gentleman and poet in George MacDonald's *At the Back of the North Wind*, who becomes Diamond's benefactor and Nanny's rescuer, embodying the Victorian ideal of charitable patronage.

memes.label: Mr. Stonecrop
memes.description: A kind-hearted cab-owner who gives Diamond's father work and a horse after the Coleman family's downfall.

memes.label: Mrs. Coleman
memes.description: The wife of Mr. Coleman and mother of Miss Coleman, a lady of the Coleman household whose fortunes rise and fall with her husband's.

memes.label: Mrs. Crump
memes.description: A housekeeper employed by Mr. Raymond who takes in Diamond and his family during their time of hardship, later accompanying the household to Hoxton.

memes.label: Mrs. Raymond
memes.description: The wife of Mr. Raymond, a kind-hearted woman who, with her husband, provides a home for Diamond in the novel's closing chapters.

memes.label: Music
memes.description: A pervasive motif in George MacDonald's *At the Back of the North Wind*, where music serves as a bridge between the earthly and the divine — from Diamond's spontaneous songs to the transcendent "far-off song" heard by the North Wind.

memes.label: Nanny
memes.description: A poor crossing-sweeper girl in George MacDonald's *At the Back of the North Wind*, befriended and helped by Diamond, whose transformation through kindness illustrates the novel's themes of charity and moral redemption.

memes.label: Narrative Mirroring — Diamond and Princess Daylight
memes.description: A comparative analysis of the narrative doubling between Diamond and Princess Daylight — two child protagonists whose parallel journeys of confinement and liberation form the structural backbone of At the Back of the North Wind.

relations.label: the country at the back of the north wind

memes.label: North Wind
memes.description: A powerful supernatural being in George MacDonald's *At the Back of the North Wind*, who takes the form of a beautiful woman of variable size and befriends the young protagonist Diamond.

memes.label: Old Diamond
memes.description: The Diamond family's original cab-horse, a quiet and good-natured animal after whom young Diamond was named. Sold and bought back during the family's financial struggles, he remained a steadfast presence throughout the novel.

memes.label: Places by Setting
memes.description: Places in the North Wind universe organized by setting type — homes, natural landscapes, urban locations, and symbolic spaces.

memes.label: Princess Daylight
memes.description: The heroine of Mr. Raymond's embedded fairy tale, a princess cursed to sleep by day and wake only by moonlight — an allegory of the soul's relationship to light and darkness.

memes.label: Ruby
memes.description: A red horse purchased by Diamond's father as a second cab-horse, named after the ruby colour of its coat. Mentioned in connection with a ruby ring that appears in Nanny's story.

memes.label: Sal
memes.description: An elderly, gin-drinking woman who keeps Nanny as a crossing-sweeper in a London cellar, representing the harsh realities of child exploitation in Victorian London.

memes.label: Sandwich
memes.description: The historic Kent port town, once a seaport but landlocked by silting, where Diamond briefly stays — a place associated with rest and seashore visits.

memes.label: Sleep
memes.description: The liminal state through which Diamond accesses the dream-world of North Wind; both a literal biological act and a metaphor for the passage between life and death.

relations.label: the country at the back of the north wind

memes.label: Song
memes.description: Song as a mode of spiritual communication and healing — Diamond's primary means of ministering to others and expressing his visionary experience.

memes.label: Spatial Theology — The Ascent from Mews to the Back of the North Wind
memes.description: An analysis of the novel's spatial hierarchy — from the cramped mews of London to the boundless country at the back of the north wind — as a theological geography in which every place is a station on the soul's ascent.

memes.label: stars
memes.description: The stars as a central celestial motif in George MacDonald's *At the Back of the North Wind*.

memes.label: Story World
memes.description: A comprehensive hub of the story world of At the Back of the North Wind — all characters, places, and concepts in their narrative context.

memes.label: Suffering
memes.description: Physical and spiritual suffering as the novel's central problem — the reality that MacDonald's theology must explain and that Diamond's ministry exists to alleviate.

memes.label: The Cathedral
memes.description: A medieval cathedral where North Wind leaves Diamond during a storm in George MacDonald's *At the Back of the North Wind* — a liminal space where he encounters moonlight, organ music, and the mystery of the eastern window.

memes.label: The Church
memes.description: The church near Diamond's home where the family worships and where Diamond's spiritual nature is contextualized within conventional Victorian Christianity.

memes.label: The Cottage
memes.description: Mr. Raymond's country cottage where Diamond's family, Nanny, Jim, and Mrs. Crump live together during the novel's peaceful final chapters.

relations.label: the country at the back of the north wind

memes.label: The Country
memes.description: The countryside destination where Mr. Raymond relocates Diamond's family and the setting of the novel's peaceful final chapters, contrasted with and shadowed by the mystical "country at the back of the north wind."

relations.label: the country at the back of the north wind

memes.label: The Crossing
memes.description: The street crossings of London where poor children like Nanny and Jim work as crossing-sweepers, clearing paths for pedestrians in exchange for pennies.

memes.label: The Garden
memes.description: The garden at Mr. Raymond's country cottage, where Diamond spends his final peaceful days and which serves as an earthly image of Paradise.

relations.label: the country at the back of the north wind

memes.label: The Hay-Loft
memes.description: Diamond's childhood bedroom in George MacDonald's *At the Back of the North Wind* — a hay-loft above the stable where the north wind blows through the chinks in the wall and the stars look in through the roof.

memes.label: The Hospital
memes.description: The charitable hospital for sick children that Mr. Raymond supports, where Diamond visits the suffering and which represents institutionalized compassion in the novel.

memes.label: The Mews
memes.description: The mews building where Diamond's family lives in rented rooms — a stable-converted lodging house that is the setting for much of the novel's domestic action.

memes.label: the-mound
memes.description: "The country estate of Mr. Raymond, built upon an artificial hill, where Diamond serves as a page in the later chapters of *At the Back of the North Wind*."

memes.label: the River
memes.description: A recurring liminal setting in the novel where Diamond encounters North Wind and crosses between the waking world and the dream-country.

relations.label: the country at the back of the north wind

memes.label: The Sea
memes.description: The sea as it appears throughout the novel — a real geographical feature visited by Diamond and his mother, a metaphor for death and the unknown, and a landscape of North Wind's domain.

memes.label: the Stable
memes.description: The stable-yard where Diamond lives with his family and where his father works as a coachman; the earthly anchor-point from which Diamond's dream-journeys begin.

memes.label: The Street
memes.description: The London streets where Diamond, Nanny, and Jim work and wander — the novel's primary setting of urban poverty, child labor, and providential encounters.

memes.label: Trust
memes.description: The childlike trust that Diamond exemplifies — an unquestioning confidence in the goodness of North Wind and, through her, of the divine order itself.

memes.label: Truth
memes.description: A philosophical and moral concept in George MacDonald's *At the Back of the North Wind*, where truth is presented not merely as factual accuracy but as alignment with a deeper spiritual reality — and where the novel's child protagonist becomes an unlikely philosopher of truth.

memes.label: Wonder
memes.description: A mode of perception central to George MacDonald's *At the Back of the North Wind*, where wonder functions as the child's natural lens through which the ordinary world reveals its extraordinary depths.
